title: Grid cell residue number system mapped to QA modular arithmetic

claims.label: Grid cell phase code is a residue number system isomorphic to QA

witnesses.isomorphism: Both compute state as residue modulo a period/modulus

claims.label: CRT reconstruction: grid cell decoding = QA multi-modulus join

witnesses.grid_crt: Position uniquely recoverable from (x mod lambda_1, ..., x mod lambda_N) via CRT
witnesses.coprime_capacity: prod(lambda_i) distinguishable positions when moduli coprime
witnesses.example: For moduli (13,15,16,17,19): capacity = 1,007,760 positions from 5 residues

claims.label: QA ratio 24/9 = 2.667 within 2% of optimal e = 2.718

witnesses.derivation: Minimize N = d * r * log_r(R) over r; dN/dr = 0 implies ln(r) = 1, r = e
witnesses.qa_note: 24/9 is the ratio of QA applied modulus to theoretical modulus

witnesses.grid_cell_analog: Non-coprime modules have capacity = LCM, not product
witnesses.qa_note: 72 is exactly the number of distinct (b,e) pairs in the 24-cycle Cosmos orbit

claims.label: Golden ratio phi optimal for two-module coding; QA norm field = Q(sqrt(5))

witnesses.optimal_for: Two-module grid cell RNS (Hurwitz theorem bound)
witnesses.qa_norm: f(b,e) = b*b + b*e - e*e = N(b + e*phi) in Q(sqrt(5))
witnesses.connection: QA is algebraically built on the number phi that is optimal for two-module residue coding

witnesses.grid_property: Each module updates phase independently: (a mod lambda) + (b mod lambda) = (a+b) mod lambda
witnesses.qa_property: QA state (b,e) updates within modulus m without cross-modulus communication
witnesses.computational_advantage: No inter-module coordination needed; parallel updates
witnesses.source: Fiete et al. 2008; Kymn et al. Neural Computation 37:1, 2025

witnesses.experiment: Subjects learned 2D bird space (neck length x leg length); fMRI showed 6-fold hexagonal symmetry
witnesses.session_stability: Grid angles persist across hours and weeks
witnesses.qa_note: Supports QA application to non-spatial domains (finance, EEG, audio, climate)

claims.label: Toroidal state space: grid modules on tori = QA pairs on (Z/mZ)^2

witnesses.grid_topology: Each grid module fires on a hexagonal lattice with periodic boundary = 2D torus
witnesses.qa_topology: QA state pairs (b,e) in (Z/mZ)^2 form a discrete 2D torus
witnesses.orbit_partition: Three QA orbits (Cosmos, Satellite, Singularity) partition the torus into invariant subsets

claims.label: Hexagonal encoding for m=9 uses 27 codebook vectors

witnesses.qa_note: 27 = 3 x 9 = three enneads (Hebrew/Greek letter-number system); 217 = 7 x 31 (Ashe Seven Palaces sum)

falsifiers.description: If 24/9 deviated more than 10% from e, the economy-principle connection would be weak
